from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "genre": "musical genre is {obj}",
        "instrument": "plays {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is a member of the band {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "is signed to {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started their career in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended their career in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "has its stylistic origins in {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "is fused with {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "has a derivative {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        # leader is handled specially to place the object as the subject.
        "leader": "SPECIAL_LEADER",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "is a {obj}",
    }

    # Aggregate objects per subject and predicate to enable natural list phrasing.
    from collections import defaultdict

    def _join_items(items: list[str]) -> str:
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last item."""
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # subject -> predicate -> list of objects
    subject_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))

    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        subject_pred_objs[subj][pred].append(obj)

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, pred_dict in subject_pred_objs.items():
        clause_parts: list[str] = []

        # Combine genre and instrument into a natural phrase if both exist.
        if "genre" in pred_dict and "instrument" in pred_dict:
            genre_objs = _join_items(pred_dict.pop("genre"))
            instrument_objs = _join_items(pred_dict.pop("instrument"))
            # e.g., "plays the piano in the Psychedelia genre"
            combined = f"plays {instrument_objs} in the {genre_objs} genre"
            clause_parts.append(combined)

        for pred, objs in pred_dict.items():
            # Special handling for predicates where object should become subject.
            if pred == "musicFusionGenre":
                obj_str = _join_items(objs)
                clause = f"{obj_str} is a fusion genre of {subj}"
                clause_parts.append(clause)
                continue
            if pred == "derivative":
                obj_str = _join_items(objs)
                clause = f"{obj_str} is a derivative of {subj}"
                clause_parts.append(clause)
                continue
            if pred == "leader":
                obj_str = _join_items(objs)
                clause = f"{obj_str} is the leader of {subj}"
                clause_parts.append(clause)
                continue

            template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
            # Join multiple objects into a natural phrase.
            joined_objs = _join_items(objs)
            # If the template expects {obj}, substitute the joined string.
            if "{obj}" in template:
                clause = template.format(obj=joined_objs)
            else:
                clause = f"{pred} {joined_objs}"
            clause_parts.append(clause)

        # Combine clauses for the same subject.
        if len(clause_parts) == 1:
            body = clause_parts[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
